content:
- AVOID SPECULATION
The economic calendar for today and tomorrow shows Chairman Powell testifying at 10 o'clock both days.
In my opinion, you should not be trying to speculate this morning. No one has any idea, I don't have an
idea that is high probability and low risk when the FED is involved. All things go out the window, my
Concepts included. It's manual intervention, meaning they're going to do whatever they're going to do,
period. It is not a lack of liquidity as sometimes people will say; it is absolutely controlled demolition.
Because of that, I tell my students that this is a time when you want to sit on your hands and do nothing. Be
content with not knowing and give yourself permission: I don't know what they're going to do this morning, I
have no bias, you shouldn't have a bias this morning either.

section_title: OBSERVING MARKET BEHAVIOR DURING FED EVENTS
content:
What you're doing is looking to see how the testimony that the FED chairman gives during the 10 o'clock
hour, and what they leave in the wake of that, meaning what the market leaves in terms of liquidity and
what the market leaves in terms of inefficiency. Because they're the only two factors that the market isn't
considering:
- It's either going to go up for buy stops or reprice to an inefficiency like a fair value Gap that would be
above price.
- Or it's going to go down below old lows or sell side liquidity, or go lower to an inefficiency like a fair value
Gap below market price.
So what you're doing is you're going to be observing this morning the effects of what that chairman can do.
He's not pushing price around; that's not what's actually happening here. It's not a lack of liquidity because
he's talking. The market is going through a series of manual interventions where they're going to run to
levels that may or may not be expected by you.

section_title: RISKS OF TRADING DURING FED MANUAL INTERVENTION
content:
Every time that I've ever done this, trading during these periods, I've never felt comfortable, even if I was
right, I did not feel comfortable. Whereas any other time, I'm not influenced by whatever the Market's doing;
I'm not scared of it, I'm not afraid, I'm not clueless, lost in the charts. I know what I'm looking for. But
because there's a manual intervention when fed is involved, that means everything and all the rules go out
the window for that period of time.

Once that testimony is done, a couple minutes, maybe 15 minutes after that, the lay of the land, whatever
the chart shows after that, that's what I utilize for the PM session. So speculation in the morning session
really, it's just a Gamble. And if you win, it would be foolish for you to attribute that as skill. And to speculate
during this time period and willingly open yourself up to risk when you have no idea what it's going to do,
you have no idea where they're going to take it, you have no idea how they're going to do it. I don't know
what profile they would be using today, it means like a schematic or a road map, what they would run for,
which is how I use narrative, like when I look at Price action, I'm outlining things to you. The idea of how
markets book, where they go, why they go this level first then that level second, that's all narrative. And I
don't have a way of knowing the influence when the FED steps in.

section_title: RESPECTING RISK AND OBSERVATION VS. GAMBLING
content:
To respect that risk and also to communicate that to you as a student, it's important for you to know that
you should not think that you should know what to do this morning. To do so, to believe that is foolish;
you're literally gambling, that's the definition of gambling because you don't know what the Market's going
to do this morning. Whereas any other time, I have a pretty good inclination about where markets are going
to go and I think I've proven it so far for a number of years now. But there's no weakness in saying that I
don't know it right now because it's the truth and no one else does either. So you can watch other people
out there gamble, whether they're in live streams or come back after the fact and show you this is what my
system, this is what I did as a Trader, and call it live. If you're not doing it live, it didn't happen. And I don't
have any shame in telling you that as your Mentor, this is a time when I don't know. I don't know, there's no
secret I'm holding back. There's no method or model that works perfectly in times like this. I don't have that,
so I simply step aside and let the market do whatever it's going to do and let the Carnage unfold. And as I
like to tell my long-term students, let the gamblers sacrifice themselves on the altar of chance. It's the same
thing that goes on when it's the non-farm payroll, which is this week too. So you're looking at a very, very
difficult terrain as a Trader this week.

section_title: MORNING OBSERVATION AND PM SESSION FOCUS
content:
I'm just going to let this mark some time until we get into 10 o'clock. I'm going to try not to do too much
talking today. I'm going to be observing anything that's noteworthy. I'll talk about anything that is not
noteworthy to me; I won't mention. So it's more or less for you to observe real time with me and what I find
is useful, what's informative rather, that I may reflect on in the PM session. The PM session is 1:30 to 4
o'clock New York local time. So I'm not interested in pushing any buttons this morning, neither should you.
The foolish thing for you to do is to say, 'Well, since I wouldn't be trading, I'm not going to pay attention and
watch the price action.' That would be foolish of you because it's important for you to understand how the
market behaves when the FED steps in. So when it's pertinent, I'll mention certain things; if it's not, I'll
remain quiet.

- DXY AND NASDAQ
So far this morning, dollar Index has moved up into a buy side liquidity pool. If you look at your high
yesterday on dxy, the high comes in at 104.691. We've already swept that this morning. NASDAQ has
swept its relative equal lows down here. So we've had that.

Now for the purposes of study, what I do when I'm looking at the market like this and I'm not pushing a
button this morning, the exercise I go through when I'm looking at like non-farm payroll, or if I'm looking at
FOMC, or if there is a testimony like this with the Fed chair, what I like to do is I like to annotate where I
think if I were in complete control of the marketplace, where would I personally steer price to and why. So
what that means is I annotate the 5 and 15 minute chart buy side liquidity pools, in other words the highs
that haven't been returned to. And cell side below here. So those two levels I would be watching to see
which one of those levels does the FED influence first.

section_title: HYPOTHETICAL PRICE PATH AND FAIR VALUE GAPS
content:
Now because we've approached this low here and didn't take it, and at 10 o'clock, if I were running things, I
would take it up here first, and then come down here, and maybe even as far as down into this Fair Day
Gap over here. It does not mean that's what's going to happen. Please don't read into that and don't come
back later on and say, 'Oh, he got it wrong.' I'm just telling you what I'm watching on a day I wouldn't be
trading anyway. So it's 10 o'clock. We are in this new week opening Gap here for this particular week we're
trading in now. The next downside would be here. If we get to the midpoint between both, it would be
reasonable to expect it to reprice down to here. Dollar Index is expanding higher, so the risk off is the flavor
of the morning this morning.

Pound dollar for those that are in Forex, that was really taking a dive from 1.1985 going to 43s, 42s now off
of a fair value Gap. You can find that fair value Gap on your five minute chart for pound dollar at the 8:50
Candle on the five minute chart. Dollar Index next pool of liquidity on the buy side would be on the 10 a.m
from Friday of last week, and that high comes in at 105. We're in Striking Distance of that now. So we've
reached the midpoint between one new opening Gap to this one here. So your expectation should be
repricing to here.

Look how the bodies are respected. I'm teaching you how to use gaps for real, not just 'oh, there's a gap
there, it might go there.' I tell you how to use them, and once they go there, I'm not done with them where
everybody else is. That's the difference, that's the context, and that's why it's still trending on Twitter. If it's
something that's always been around, the people that have used it before wouldn't show the excitement
they've now garnered with it. I've never heard anybody talk about using a weekly Gap as a means of
mapping out what the Market's going to go to the next one too. That's okay, I understand the 'ethereality
complex,' this has crept into this industry a lot.

So far we had about 20 handles or so down time right at 10 o'clock. It's important not to look at these types
of moves and think that 'oh, I wish I would have gotten some of that.' This is a way of forging patience and
self-control. Moves like this happen a lot without the requirement of the FED being a factor in it. When you
introduce the fed, you introduce uncertainty. You don't know what side's gonna happen first. And to be
wrong on a day like this, it can move against you quickly, and a lot of you that are new or afraid to use stop
losses, unfortunately, it's not something you should be doing. You end up hurting yourself by not
understanding the risks involved in a day like today.

All right, so Dollar Index is now running. It's already gone through its 105 buy stop. So the next buy side
liquidity pool for a dollar, still showing risk off, would be the high on March 2nd, Thursday. That high being
105.18. It's about to pierce through that now. Next level of interest we've had on Dollar was 105.341 and
then 105.631. So if my memory serves me correct, this is the old high, consequent encroachment, and
then the rejection block on the daily chart.

section_title: CHASING PRICE AND NEW WEEK OPENING GAP NARRATIVE
content:
On the one minute chart, you can see that you already had an opportunity once it starts moving, it's over.
Anything in here shorting would be chasing. I would require at least one up close candle to get in sync with
it if it was something like on a day that I expected a low resistance liquidity run. But because there's no
retracement, you just got to let it go. And again, I'm only comparing and contrasting that with what another
day would be, opposed to like what we're seeing the FED having an influence today. If it was another day
where your Fed chair wasn't jabbering, in this movement here, I would be trying to get short, but I would
require an up close candle to do so. And there's your difference between a weekly open Gap that
everybody knows about in trading. Yes, but you don't ever hear anybody talk about what I've been teaching
you, which is taking them and stringing them together for a narrative. So moving from one new week
opening Gap to a previous new opening. And this has been traded to and through multiple times, but it's
acting like a magnet because it's algorithmic. The Market's going to reprice to that for the purposes of fair
value. Has nothing to do with buying and selling pressure. If you can accelerate to the downside, we have
one down here, but it doesn't need to go all the way down to that. Between both of them, midpoint, that's
my event horizon. We watch to see, does it have a wellness to get down to at least the midpoint of it.

- REJECTION BLOCK AND VOLUME IMBALANCE
Let's go with the dollar real quick. Okay, so it's traded back above. And let's go into the daily chart. It's the
rejection block here, that's what that dotted line is. And the next line was going to be the consequent
encouragement of the wick. And everything resumes back to this is halfway of this Wick here, which is
constant encouragement, means the old high. And this is where I've mentioned that I feel that dollar could
be trying to get back up into, which is the volume imbalance on the daily chart. So it looks like that, so we
had this really drawn out consolidation, which you know is frustrating, but we're looking for it to get up into
longer term here, which I mean bearishness on equities and on foreign currencies.

section_title: ICT METHODOLOGY VS. CONVENTIONAL WISDOM ON GAPS
content:
Talk about a Sunday Gap being referred to after it's been used multiple times and weeks ago, you don't see
that, folks. Sorry, it's not to hurt anybody's feelings, but that's just the reality of it all. It's not rehashed, it's
not rebranded, it's not anything except for the real logic behind what makes these markets move price. If
that hurt your feelings or upset you, you're going to have to go through that.

- DXY TARGETS REACHED
So we're at that crossroads right now, where dollar has reached into a buy side liquidity pool, back into its
rejection block from the daily chart. It's done so in a straight shot, one run straight up, one direction. And we
have pierced and traded through the lower, close proximity new week opening gap here. So this is the
moment we've been working for this week, now we're here. And yes, I have a teaching that I'll be producing
for this week on YouTube with today's opening guide. So we have some rules to work with.

- DOW AS BAROMETER, SMT DIVERGENCE
Just one quick look at Pound Dollar, which I know a lot of you guys are Forex Traders. We have to figure out
how you got there, I mentioned earlier, and dovishness on the dollar really took a dive. I don't trade Dow, I
like to use Dow as a barometer, much like I use the dollar Index with every other Market. When I'm looking
at the indices NASDAQ or ES, I'm referring to Dow for the purposes of SMT, comparing highs and lows
together. See how flat that is, comparatively, and look at NASDAQ, there's respective highs. And then again
Dow, ES. You see the divergence and you see the weakness.

Price has been delivered algorithmically, yes, but with a manual intervention. So they push it real quick,
sudden, one direction. And my question to you is, you're saying that the market ran out of liquidity and just
happened to go randomly down into an old Sunday gap opening? Is that what you're saying, that's your
argument? It makes no sense. Okay, it's controlled, everything's controlled. Even when they're intervening
manually, they're taking it to levels that I'm going to teach you exist, but the problem is is I don't know which
one they're going to go to because they're intervening manually. Because I don't know with a great deal of
probability how to anticipate what they're going to do. Because many times whatever the FED chairman
would say, even if the rate announcement is in line or not in line with what everyone expects, because you
see like in calendars and fundamental websites, they'll say 'If the Fed is hawkish or dovish, this would
cause this to happen in the marketplace,' and then when it comes out, it comes to either expectation or
misses the expectation over whatever they think is going to happen with rates, and then the opposite
actually unfolds. Well, then they'll say, 'Well, that's because it was already priced in weeks ago,' but they
didn't say that before that. So they're looking for an excuse to justify something that's already happened. I'm
looking for a signature to justify why I think price is going to go somewhere. And I'm usually right on these
that are not having influence like this, and that's where I try to focus. I try to teach my students to focus
there, that way the probabilities are in your favor, at least in my favor as the analyst.

And if the analyst isn't correct, the trader that's using the analysis isn't going to be accurate either. So it
goes back to knowing what you're looking for, when you're looking for it, and this, is that environment that
you're trying to speculate in or observing, is it conducive for high probability in the first place? And
whenever you have the Fed talking, speaking, testifying, it's equivalent to a rate announcement where you
don't know the effects on the marketplace. You can all have 'oh, they're going to cut rates,' 'they're not
going to cut rates,' or 'increase rates.' Everybody has an opinion, but that opinion means nothing. It has no
bearing on what markets are going to do, period. They're going to book the way they want to book, and it
matters not how many people are buying and selling.

Dollar Index making a higher high here. So we would want to see that low taken out on ES, that hasn't
happened yet, but dollar has made a higher high. So if this doesn't make the lower low, we might be seeing
a short-term low forming in ES and trade up a little bit. Dollar is coming off the highs. And ES can attempt
to reach into a little bit deeper in this Gap right here. I'm looking at the dollar index in a five minute chart
and you scroll into a 15-minute chart in an hourly chart. And I'm monitoring, does it continuously provide to
you that it wants to keep going higher? So far it hasn't made a higher high than the high it's already
produced, which is problematic for ES continuing at least near term on the downside. So we're just
watching and see what we get in terms of delivery on ES. Also, if you look at the Dow Jones, Dow futures,
YMH2023, it's made a lower low where ES has not done so either. So whenever we're looking at that, this
gives reason to be concerned about either consolidation or a potential short-term reversal.

Many times when I'm like, say this is a day where it wasn't the Fed and I was short already, whenever I see
situations like this, like the Dow making the lower low, NASDAQ failing to make the lower low, and ES not
making the lower low either, to me, I would want to take more of the position off because it could mean it
could create a retracement higher against the position that would have open. So these are all things that I
weigh in the balance as price is delivering. Dow has still not had its higher high since it's formed it. So far
the high on today is 105.265. I would want to see a continuing higher in the dollar to get to 105.341 and in
a lower low in ES and that would bring things back to being symmetrical. So it means everything being
equal: higher high in dollar, lower lows in all index features like ES, NASDAQ, and Dow. Watch how price is
utilizing this Old New Week Opening Gap.

If you're new, how do you know you're new? You look at this move on the chart and you think, 'I wish I
could have got some of that.' That's infancy, that's someone that's not been around long enough to know
that things repeat. All right, dollar Index has just dipping below that 105.181. If dollar slips lower, say the
dollar starts to come back down into its rally up, and trades into like 104.90 something like that, I would be
watching to see this fair value Gap become support and maybe run up into this area here. So it would be
like this. And it's not a trade, it's just something to observe. This is what I do on days like this. I'm not
pushing a button, not even a demo. It would draw up into this area here, preferably coming back down in
and touching the fair value Gap here at support. Dollars slipping in here.

section_title: REAL-TIME APPLICATION OF DIVERGENCE FOR TRADE MANAGEMENT
content:
Now it was real time that I walked you through the idea of those lows not being confirmed. Remember I
was talking about if I was in a trade, I'd have most of my position off. And this is generally where I'd be
getting stopped out. My stop would be about this high here, trailed. You know, if it was a day not because
we're in Fed, I'm not trading. But if it were any other day and I'm short, my stop would have been trailed
here because of that reluctance of seeing lower lows in ES and NASDAQ while the Dow did make the low.
And if you missed it, just watch the recording and go back and you'll see me explain that and show you the
charts. If it runs to this area here without returning to this, that would be a day or that would be a trade that
I would miss if it was any other day because I would absolutely, if it wasn't Fed today, if it would have came
down to this level here, I would have said no, watch this as a potential long to here. I wouldn't do it today,
but if it was any other day, I would be taking that trade, maybe 10 handle run.

- BUY SIDE IMBALANCE AND CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT
Now we have a buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency, meaning from this candle's high and this candle's
low, the one candle in the middle, all that big movement here. You're looking at this high, that low. Between
these two reference points is consequent encouragement. Because it's an inefficiency or a gap. If it was an
order block, it would be mean threshold, but this is consequent encroachment. 4024 and a quarter.

section_title: ES PRICE ACTION AND DXY CONFLUENCE
content:
So that's consequent encroachment. Now we have two things here as a discount. This one here is the first
one, the second one is the fair value Gap I mentioned. If it returned back to it without touching this area
here, on another day, not today, I would utilize that as a long to get up to here for about a 10 handle run.
We've repriced all the way through this buy side inefficiency and we're in the fair value Gap. So watch and
see if it delivers from here up to here. If you have the benefit of toggling between Dixie or DXY, do that on
your one minute chart. You would want to see dollar Index not rally; it can stay consolidated or preferably
go lower into its buy side or sell side inefficiency on its five minute chart candle at 10 am.

Watch this wick. We wouldn't want to see it trade below midpoint at consequent encroachment. It can touch
the fair value Gap now but I'd rather see it not do that because it's already done it. I can do that but I saw
him down, so some efficiencies low compared like that. The risk would be on this candle as well. See
where the bodies are forming. The Wicks can do the damage, they can trade outside the lines. The bodies
of the candles, that's where I'm monitoring. I'm watching all that.

section_title: DXY 1-MINUTE FAIR VALUE GAP AND BEARISH BREAKER
content:
Dollar Index has a fair value Gap at one minute 10:21 candle. That's like the last line of defense for me
because it's inside the breaker for dollar. If you look at the down closed candle on the one minute DXY, that
candle being at 10:12. So it can trade up into the fair value Gap that's at the 10:21 one minute Candle on
the Dixie and inside that gap is a bearish breaker, which would be 10:12s one minute candle. It's inside that
now. So far it's giving me feedback. There's the gap, there's the breaker, so you can see it real time. And
that's five handles so far. It hasn't even taken out the short term high yet either. A little bit of a struggling
point for Dollar here. My eyes are on that fair value gap and bearish breaker on the dollar Index on its one
minute chart I just showed you. As long as you don't Vault above that, we should have no problem. So
there's the outline of a real real-time trade idea. Did you need to see a button being pushed? No. But the
logic is there. If it's not there, I would fail. You wouldn't be seeing me calling it, outlining it, and delivering
again.

I have this consequent encouragement, it's not really a consequent encouragement, but it's the level of fib
projection off of this here. And also if we measure the consequent encroachment of that sell side
imbalance. Nobody's excited, nobody's surprised, can you hear any animation? It's always no, the old cat
stuff, same stuff over the last three decades. So consequent encouragement of this sell side imbalance
would be this novel here. But from this low to that high, and Dollar has really slipped lower as we were
expecting it to do. So that's probably random. So there's the breaker outline we talked about, and inside
that breaker you know the fair value gap I was mentioning.

section_title: ANATOMY OF INTERMARKET RELATIONSHIPS AND ES TRADE IDEA
content:
So that's the anatomy of inter-market relationships and how we look at one thing versus the other. We clear
the important level that we mentioned before, which is the rejection block on the daily chart, that's what this
dashed line is. Go back and look at old recordings and you'll see that I've been talking about that, that's not
just because today, I guess the daily chart PD array, it's important you'll refer to it again. This down close
candle between the high here and this high here. Remember this is swing High one, swing High two.
Between swing High one and two, the low in between, that's the bearish breaker. It's a breaker because we
went below it here and have taken buy side. Then the market comes back up, trades into what? An
imbalance inside the breaker. Lower dollar means what? Risk on. Risk on means what for ES? Higher. Just
random higher? No, we have to get a measurement. My eye was looking at this high to that low, midpoint in
that area. First imbalance was here, there's no fair value gaps in here. So I took your right attention here.
And then if we got over zealous on the upside and because of the breaker on dollar, that means using
swing projections from the fib anchor to that low to that high takes us up to this level here. They may not get
there, I don't care if it does. I'm not going to make anything, I'm not going to lose anything. But I'm showing
you the logic if this was a day where the FED wasn't impacting price action. If they're not intervening, then I
would be in there doing that trade. You watched me outline here, trading what, the returns at the fair value
gap here. My stop would be down there. So there you go.

- PARTIAL PROFIT TAKING AT PREMIUM
You would want to be taking partials here, because you're inside the lower bracket up between this candle's
High and the fib projection and the consequent encroachment. So you're encroaching on a real premium in
the marketplace. So you want to distribute your long positions at times when the market is at premium. We
don't need an overbought indicator to indicate whether one of the overbought or oversold; we're looking at
the range itself. Does that make sense to you? You see how it reacted down here? It went twice, but look at
the body staying inside between these two groups. So in between those two heavy blue lines, the bodies
are telling you the narrative that it's going to respect this imbalance and then reprice to a premium. What's
the premium? Near term, is this high. Then it's the imbalance here, which is the sell side imbalance in the
form of a fair value Gap, sell side imbalance buy side inefficiency between those candles low, that candle's
high, that little movement on that one candle, that's what I highlighted. So I went back up into that. And then
we have this area in here, between this midpoint of that candle's highlight, I'm sorry, that candle's low and
that candle's High, that's what that measurement is, it's half of it. But the projection from this low up to this
High just with the fib, I'm trying to build a Confluence of levels to justify how far this retracement can go.
When really on a day like this, you would have the bulk of it off inside that first premium right here with that
fair value Gap.

Does that make sense? Yes sir. So this is a run from here to that high. So this level here at 4022, just
getting into this old high back here, is nine and a half handles. It's not, it's not even taking out the buy side
to get nine and a half handles. So if you have a limit order right above here for your first function, let's say
you had three contracts long, you could take one off on a limit here right at that high, right at the high. So
you're going to give up the trophy of having even 10 handles, but you don't care, that's not how you do it.
Because you have to make sure to factor in the difference between a bid and the ask, that allows you to
exit. And then you would take off the remaining two here, where at the first low, this candle's high. You
would but you're going to raise that level as soon as it hits it. If it digs into that range one tick above it, your
limit order to sell would be a market order to sell at the market. So then you would have two contracts of
the three you bought at the Fairway gap down here. And then aim for either the midpoint or the upper end
of the fair value Gap less one tick, because if it trades and prints the candle's low price here, which is the
high, that shaded area, you want to be one tick below that to allow for the bid-ask spread to allow your limit
order to get you off on that last third contract. You follow me? Yes sir.

And I think my friends and neighbors, that's going to be it for today because if I talk anymore I won't be able
to talk tomorrow, and we'll have another round of all this tomorrow because of the FED chair that I'm talking
at 10 o'clock. I'll give you a real quick look at the dollar Index. If you found today's live stream and
commentary productive and informative, give me a thumbs up. It is a Futures Contract. If you found today's
live stream helpful and you were able to track this real time with this, and if it was informative to you, I
appreciate a thumbs up. It's free to do, it doesn't cost you anything. I promise no one knows you can do it or
have done it, it's all private. It is your way of letting me know that you appreciate what I'm doing here
because I don't have to be doing this, and I'm enjoying it, but I just want to make sure I'm getting the
feedback. And if you're on Twitter, you'd like to follow me on Twitter, let me know how you figured today
following along in this real-time commentary. Final real quick, we'll just pass through the other NBC. So it's
traded up into its sell side imbalance and its fair value gap that I didn't know, but it would be comparable.
And then you have the Dow Jones, which is the weaker of them. No imbalances, and you see that none of
these candles in your head had imbalance. And then we traded just like we just fell shorter than the high at
the 364. The high comes in at 363, so it never made its way up into this sell side imbalance. So that is
going to be it today. I appreciate you hanging out with us, and until I'll talk to you tomorrow, Lord willing, be
safe.
